moduleDescription: This Module implements types and macros to facilitate the wrapping of, and interaction with JavaScript libraries. Using the provided types JsObject and JsAssoc together with the provided macros allows for smoother interfacing with JavaScript, allowing for example quick and easy imports of JavaScript variables:

entries.name: toJsKey
entries.code:
proc toJsKey[T: SomeInteger](text: cstring; t: type T): T {.
    importjs: "parseInt(#)", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: toJsKey
entries.code: proc toJsKey[T: enum](text: cstring; t: type T): T

entries.name: toJsKey
entries.code: proc toJsKey(text: cstring; t: type cstring): cstring

entries.name: toJsKey
entries.code:
proc toJsKey[T: SomeFloat](text: cstring; t: type T): T {.
    importjs: "parseFloat(#)", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: JsKey
entries.code:
JsKey = concept atypeof(T)
    cstring.toJsKey(T) is T

entries.name: JsObject
entries.code: JsObject = ref object of JsRoot
entries.description: Dynamically typed wrapper around a JavaScript object.

entries.name: JsAssoc
entries.code: JsAssoc[K; V] = ref object of JsRoot
entries.description: Statically typed wrapper around a JavaScript object.

entries.name: js
entries.code: js = JsObject

entries.description: JavaScript's __dirname pseudo-variable.

entries.description: JavaScript's __filename pseudo-variable.

entries.code:
proc isNull[T](x: T): bool {.noSideEffect, importjs: "(# === null)", raises: [],
                             tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Checks if a value is exactly null.

entries.name: isUndefined
entries.code:
proc isUndefined[T](x: T): bool {.noSideEffect, importjs: "(# === undefined)",
                                  raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Checks if a value is exactly undefined.

entries.name: hasOwnProperty
entries.code:
proc hasOwnProperty(x: JsObject; prop: cstring): bool {.

entries.description: Checks, whether x has a property of name prop.

entries.code:

entries.description: Returns the name of the JsObject's JavaScript type as a cstring.

entries.description: Turns a regular function call into an invocation of the JavaScript's new operator.

entries.code: template toJs(s: string): JsObject

entries.code:
proc `&`(a, b: cstring): cstring {.importjs: "(# + #)", raises: [], tags: [],

entries.description: Concatenation operator for JavaScript strings.

proc `[]`(obj: JsObject; field: cstring): JsObject {.importjs: "#[#]",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the value of a property of name field from a JsObject obj.

entries.code:
proc `[]`(obj: JsObject; field: int): JsObject {.importjs: "#[#]", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the value of a property of name field from a JsObject obj.

entries.code:
proc `[]=`[T](obj: JsObject; field: cstring; val: T) {.importjs: "#[#] = #",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the value of a property of name field in a JsObject obj to v.

entries.code:
proc `[]=`[T](obj: JsObject; field: int; val: T) {.importjs: "#[#] = #",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the value of a property of name field in a JsObject obj to v.

entries.code:
proc `[]`[K: JsKey; V](obj: JsAssoc[K, V]; field: K): V {.importjs: "#[#]",
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the value of a property of name field from a JsAssoc obj.

entries.code:
proc `[]=`[K: JsKey; V](obj: JsAssoc[K, V]; field: K; val: V) {.
    importjs: "#[#] = #", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the value of a property of name field in a JsAssoc obj to v.

entries.code: proc `[]`[V](obj: JsAssoc[cstring, V]; field: string): V
proc `[]=`[V](obj: JsAssoc[cstring, V]; field: string; val: V)

proc `==`(x, y: JsRoot): bool {.importjs: "(# === #)", raises: [], tags: [],
                                forbids: [].}
entries.description: Compares two JsObjects or JsAssocs. Be careful though, as this is comparison like in JavaScript, so if your JsObjects are in fact JavaScript Objects, and not strings or numbers, this is a comparison of references.

entries.code: macro `.`(obj: JsObject; field: untyped): JsObject
entries.description: Experimental dot accessor (get) for type JsObject. Returns the value of a property of name field from a JsObject x.

entries.code: macro `.=`(obj: JsObject; field, value: untyped): untyped
entries.description: Experimental dot accessor (set) for type JsObject. Sets the value of a property of name field in a JsObject x to value.

entries.code: macro `.()`(obj: JsObject; field: untyped; args: varargs[JsObject, jsFromAst]): JsObject
entries.description:
Experimental "method call" operator for type JsObject. Takes the name of a method of the JavaScript object (field) and calls it with args as arguments, returning a JsObject (which may be discarded, and may be undefined, if the method does not return anything, so be careful when using this.)

Example:

# Let's get back to the console example:
var console {.importc, nodecl.}: JsObject
let res = console.log("I return undefined!")
console.log(res) # This prints undefined, as console.log always returns
                 # undefined. Thus one has to be careful, when using
                 # JsObject calls.

entries.code: macro `.`[K: cstring; V](obj: JsAssoc[K, V]; field: untyped): V
entries.description: Experimental dot accessor (get) for type JsAssoc. Returns the value of a property of name field from a JsObject x.

entries.code: macro `.=`[K: cstring; V](obj: JsAssoc[K, V]; field: untyped; value: V): untyped
entries.description: Experimental dot accessor (set) for type JsAssoc. Sets the value of a property of name field in a JsObject x to value.

entries.code:
macro `.()`[K: cstring; V: proc](obj: JsAssoc[K, V]; field: untyped;
                                 args: varargs[untyped]): auto
entries.description: Experimental "method call" operator for type JsAssoc. Takes the name of a method of the JavaScript object (field) and calls it with args as arguments. Here, everything is typechecked, so you do not have to worry about undefined return values.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code:
iterator pairs(obj: JsObject): (cstring, JsObject) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Yields tuples of type (cstring, JsObject), with the first entry being the name of a fields in the JsObject and the second being its value wrapped into a JsObject.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items(obj: JsObject): JsObject {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Yields the values of each field in a JsObject, wrapped into a JsObject.

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys(obj: JsObject): cstring {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Yields the names of each field in a JsObject.

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[K: JsKey; V](assoc: JsAssoc[K, V]): (K, V)
entries.description: Yields tuples of type (K, V), with the first entry being a key in the JsAssoc and the second being its corresponding value.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[K, V](assoc: JsAssoc[K, V]): V
entries.description: Yields the values in a JsAssoc.

entries.name: keys
entries.code: iterator keys[K: JsKey; V](assoc: JsAssoc[K, V]): K
entries.description: Yields the keys in a JsAssoc.

entries.code: macro `{}`(typ: typedesc; xs: varargs[untyped]): auto
entries.description:
Takes a typedesc as its first argument, and a series of expressions of type key: value, and returns a value of the specified type with each field key set to value, as specified in the arguments of {}.

Example:

# Let's say we have a type with a ton of fields, where some fields do not
# need to be set, and we do not want those fields to be set to `nil`:
type
  ExtremelyHugeType = ref object
    a, b, c, d, e, f, g: int
    h, i, j, k, l: cstring
    # And even more fields ...

let obj = ExtremelyHugeType{ a: 1, k: "foo".cstring, d: 42 }

# This generates roughly the same JavaScript as:
{.emit: "var obj = {a: 1, k: "foo", d: 42};".}

entries.name: bindMethod
entries.code:
macro bindMethod(procedure: typed): auto {.
    deprecated: "Don\'t use it with closures".}
entries.description:
Takes the name of a procedure and wraps it into a lambda missing the first argument, which passes the JavaScript builtin this as the first argument to the procedure. Returns the resulting lambda.

Example:

We want to generate roughly this JavaScript:

var obj = {a: 10};
obj.someMethod = function() {
  return this.a + 42;
};

We can achieve this using the bindMethod macro:

let obj = JsObject{ a: 10 }
proc someMethodImpl(that: JsObject): int =
  that.a.to(int) + 42
obj.someMethod = bindMethod someMethodImpl

# Alternatively:
obj.someMethod = bindMethod
  proc(that: JsObject): int = that.a.to(int) + 42
